Knowledge Base

API Overview

Mnemosyne exposes multiple interfaces: Python SDK, Hermes plugin tools, and REST API.

Interface Comparison

InterfaceBest ForLatencySetup
Python SDKDirect integration, scriptsLowestpip install
Hermes PluginAgent frameworksLowPlugin config
REST APIRemote services, microservicesMediumServer start

Python SDK

The primary interface for Python applications:

from mnemosyne import Memory

mem = Memory()
mem.remember("User prefers dark mode.")
results = mem.recall("user preferences")

Hermes Plugin

First-class integration with the Hermes agent framework:

# hermes.yaml
plugins:
- name: mnemosyne
  config:
    db_path: ./memory.db
    auto_context: true

REST API

Start the server:

python -m mnemosyne.server --port 8080

Example request:

curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/remember -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"content": "User prefers dark mode.", "tags": ["preferences"]}'

Authentication

InterfaceAuth Method
Python SDKNone (local)
Hermes PluginVia Hermes auth
REST APIAPI key header (X-API-Key)

Rate Limits

InterfaceDefault Limit
Python SDKUnlimited (local)
Hermes PluginUnlimited
REST API1000 req/min

Error Handling

All interfaces return consistent error structures:

{
  "error": {
      "code": "MEMORY_NOT_FOUND",
      "message": "Memory ID mem_abc123 does not exist.",
      "status": 404,
  }
}
SDK Version Compatibility

Python SDK, Hermes plugin, and REST API share the same core version. Ensure all components use matching versions for full compatibility.
